passing_hypotheses.title: GEV Tail Index (xi) as Phylogenomic Signature of Thermal Adaptation Strategy
passing_hypotheses.mechanism: GEV shape parameter xi fitted to proteome Tm distributions classifies thermal adaptation strategy (tail truncation vs. distribution shift). Thermophiles: more negative xi (Weibull domain, compressed lower tail). Psychrophiles: xi closer to zero (Gumbel, preserved tail shape but shifted location). Testable across 13 Meltome Atlas species via xi-OGT regression.
passing_hypotheses.mathematical_framework: GEV shape parameter xi, Fisher-Tippett-Gnedenko theorem, maximum likelihood estimation
datasets: Meltome Atlas PRIDE PXD011929 (13 species, 48,000 proteins)
passing_hypotheses.validation_approach: computational — fit GEV per species, correlate xi with OGT, phylogenetically independent contrasts

passing_hypotheses.title: Complex-Minimum Tm Return Levels Predict Process-Specific Thermal Failure Temperatures
passing_hypotheses.citation_issue_detail: TPCA finding attributed to Mateus 2020 MSB (review); correct primary source is Tan et al. 2018, Science 359:1170-1177
conditions: Correct TPCA attribution from Mateus 2020 MSB to Tan et al. 2018 Science 359:1170-1177
Quantify chaperone buffering correction for in vivo vs. in vitro Tm gap
passing_hypotheses.mechanism: Return level estimation on complex-minimum Tm distributions predicts process-specific thermal failure temperatures. The 1% return level of ribosomal complex-minimum Tm predicts the temperature at which translation rate drops below 90% baseline; same framework for mitochondrial respiratory chain. Agreement within +/-2C validates framework; systematic underestimation indicates chaperone buffering.
passing_hypotheses.mathematical_framework: Return level R_p = mu + (sigma/xi)[(-log(1-p))^{-xi} - 1], GEV fitting to complex-minimum Tm, profile likelihood CIs
passing_hypotheses.validation_approach: computational + experimental: puromycin incorporation (translation), Seahorse respirometry (mitochondria)
passing_hypotheses.nearest_applied_domain: thermal physiology, heat stroke research, therapeutic hyperthermia

passing_hypotheses.title: GPD Scale Parameter Predicts Evolutionary Rate in the Thermally Vulnerable Subproteome
passing_hypotheses.citation_issue_detail: Drummond et al. 2005 cited as Cell; correct journal is PNAS 102:14338
conditions: Clearly distinguish novel component (sigma-dN/dS correlation) from prior art (GO enrichment per Leuenberger 2017)
Acknowledge and control for Drummond-identified confounders (expression level, interaction degree) in dN/dS analysis
passing_hypotheses.mechanism: GPD scale parameter sigma of lower-tail Tm exceedances (proteins with Tm below POT threshold) negatively correlates with mean dN/dS of orthologous tail-protein genes across 13 Meltome Atlas species. Small sigma = narrow vulnerability zone = strong purifying selection = low dN/dS. Large sigma = wide vulnerability spread = weaker selection = higher dN/dS.
passing_hypotheses.mathematical_framework: GPD (Generalized Pareto Distribution), peaks-over-threshold, mean residual life plot, PAML codeml for dN/dS
passing_hypotheses.validation_approach: computational — GPD fit, ortholog dN/dS via PAML, partial correlation controlling for expression level and OGT

failed_hypotheses.title: Censored GEV Recovers the Invisible 20% Below TPP Measurement Window
failed_hypotheses.fail_reason: MECHANISM IMPLAUSIBLE: censored GEV assumes all censored proteins have defined Tm, but IDPs (30-50% of eukaryotic proteome) lack cooperative unfolding transition — Tm is undefined for substantial fraction of censored population. Model misspecification by population heterogeneity.
